relative_path: Data & Figures/Data/6. Deep Visual Proteomics/Experimental Protocol Pilot in June.md
full_text:
**Project Overview:** Pilot LCM Experiment at FIMM

**Tissue IDs:** 535 iOme (Upper slice for Xenium); 537 iOme 1 (Lower
slice for Xenium),

additional test sample for the Antigen Retrieval Test S602_iOme

Additional treated blank PEN slide

**Tissue thickness:**10 µm

# 1. Experimental Timeline

- **Monday, May 25:** Procurement of supplies from FIMM, inventory
 > verification, and finalization of logistical coordination.

- **Wednesday, May 27:** Preparation of slides with the UV and
 > formulation of required reagents, protocol finalizing, start
 > preparation of reagents for the experiment

- **Thursday, May 28:** Sectioning of Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded
 > (FFPE) tissue blocks, preparation of reagents for the experiment
 > continues

- **Friday, May 29:** Tissue pre-processing, deparaffinization, and
 > immunofluorescence staining.

- **Saturday, May 31:** Fluorescence scanning and digital imaging of
 > stained sections.

- **Monday, June 1:** Preliminary systems test at the FIMM facility.

- **Tuesday, June 2:** Initiation of the formal pilot LCM experiment
 > at FIMM.

# 2. Materials & Reagents

- **Slides & Membranes:** Polyethylene naphthalate (PEN) membrane
 > slides (2.0 µm thickness).

- **Consumables:** 4titude 4TI-0960/C and 4TI-0740/C plates, Thermo
 > AB-0626 Adhesive PCR Sealing Foil Sheets, slide coverslips

- **Pipette tips:**

slides (2.0 µm thickness).

- **Consumables:** 4titude 4TI-0960/C and 4TI-0740/C plates, Thermo
 > AB-0626 Adhesive PCR Sealing Foil Sheets, slide coverslips

- **Pipette tips:**

- **Buffers & Solutions:**

 - Xylene

 - Ethanol gradient (100%, 90%, 70%, 30%)

 - 1x Tris-buffered saline (TBS)

 - 10 mM citric acid buffer

 - Odyssey blocking buffer

 - Glycerol 70% mounting solution (prepare in TBS)

 - Antibodies (Ki67, pRb, panCK)

## Preparation of the reagents

- **Xylene**

 - Xylene in 3 boxes (glass)

- **Ethanol solutions (100%, 90%, 70%, 50%, 30%) (glass)**

 - 300 ml 100% Ethanol

 - 300 ml 90% Ethanol - 270 ml Ethanol + 30 ml MQ

 - 300 ml 70% Ethanol - 210 ml Ethanol + 90 ml MQ

 - 300 ml 50% Ethanol - 150 ml Ethanol + 150 ml MQ

 - 300 ml 30% Ethanol - 90 ml Ethanol + 210 ml MQ

- **1x Tris-buffered saline (TBS) buffer**

 - Prepare 3 L - 300 ml 10x TBS Add 2.7L MQ

 - Prepare 10x TBS (check the recipe)

- **10 mM citric acid buffer, pH 6.0 in MQ**

 - Prepare 300 ml - 30 ml 100mM citric acid into 270 ml MQ

 - *100 mM citric acid 1L:*

 - Dissolve 21.0 grams of citric acid monohydrate into 800 mL
 > of MQ H20. (wait for long time until it dissolves, mix
 > with stirring the bottle)

 - Add 9.94 grams of NaOH pellets

 - Leave on a bench for a while to dissolve, stir from time to
 > time

 - Measure the pH and set it to 6.0 (strips where the cytospin
 > are)

- Add 9.94 grams of NaOH pellets

 - Leave on a bench for a while to dissolve, stir from time to
 > time

 - Measure the pH and set it to 6.0 (strips where the cytospin
 > are)

 - Level the volume up to 1L with MQ H20.

- **Odyssey blocking buffer (take whatever found in the fridge)**

- **70% of Glycerol in 1xTBS (use vortex and swirling for stirring)**

- **milliQ water**

 - Remove the first 250 ml, we need at least 4L (to the glass
 > bottle)

# 3. Day 0: Slide Preparation & Tissue Sectioning

1. **UV Sterilization:** Place the PEN membrane slides in a tissue
 > culture hood, ensuring the membrane side is oriented upwards.
 > Remove Petri dish/tray lids and expose to UV light for exactly 60
 > minutes. *Optimal crosslinking requires UV-C exposure (254 nm, at
 > least 1 Joule).* Following sterilization, seal the slides in a
 > container with a desiccant pack.

2. **Sectioning:** Section the FFPE tissue blocks at a thickness of 10
 > µm and mount them onto the sterilized PEN slides.

# 4. Day 1: Deparaffinization, Antigen Retrieval, & Staining

1. **Tissue Adhesion (Baking):** Incubate the mounted slides at 56°C
 > for 60 minutes to promote tissue adhesion.

2. **Deparaffinization & Rehydration (\~1 hr):**

 - Xylene washes: 3 x 5 min

 - Ethanol series: 100%, 90%, 70%, 50%, 30% for 3 min each

 - Equilibration: 1x TBS for 2 x 1 min

3.

. **Deparaffinization & Rehydration (\~1 hr):**

 - Xylene washes: 3 x 5 min

 - Ethanol series: 100%, 90%, 70%, 50%, 30% for 3 min each

 - Equilibration: 1x TBS for 2 x 1 min

3. **Heat-Induced Epitope Retrieval (HIER):**

**HIER Method Selection (To be decided):** Use 10 mM citric acid buffer.

Note: option A will be utilized if the membrane detaches from a test
empty slide

Take to FIMM 1xTBS and 3 glass racks for washes.

- **Option A:** 65°C for 30 minutes. Cool at room temperature (RT) for
 > 20 minutes, followed by 3 washes in 1x TBS.

- **Option B:** 90°C for 30 minutes. Cool at room temperature (RT) for
 > 20 minutes, followed by 3 washes in 1x TBS.

 - First slide with S602_iOme goes into the chamber

 - Then, after the run, if everything is ok, we continue with
 > Xenium slides and empty pretreated slide

**Protocol (Using 95°C Preset):**

1. Add 550 ml of MQ water to the chamber.

2. Inside the chamber, there are 3 rack holders. Add about 225 ml
 > Citric acid buffer to the **rack holder** where slides will be
 > placed. Add MQ water to the other rack holders if they will not
 > hold slides.

3. Place the slides in the rack holder containing Citric acid buffer.

4. Tighten the lid firmly, turn the device on, and select the preset
 > setting of 95°C without preheat. The total time takes around 45
 > minutes.

5.

taining Citric acid buffer.

4. Tighten the lid firmly, turn the device on, and select the preset
 > setting of 95°C without preheat. The total time takes around 45
 > minutes.

5. Once the protocol is over, open the lid carefully (use heat
 > resistance gloves) and transfer the slides and citric acid buffer
 > to a new plastic rack holder.

6. Take the slides to the hood to cool faster. 20 minutes should be
 > enough for the temperature to cool down to 40°C.

7. Wash **three times** with 1x PBS, \~3 min each.

**[Alternative HIER Protocol for future (Lengyel lab):]{.mark}**

[Antigen retrieval occurred in 1x DAKO pH9 HIER buffer (Agilent
Dako)/10% glycerol (v/v) (Sigma) in a preheated water bath at 90°C for
60 min, followed by the blocking of nonspecific binding sites using 5%
BSA in PBS for 60 min at room temperature.]{.mark}

4. **Blocking:** Prepare a humidified chamber. Carefully dry the
 > hydrophobic perimeter surrounding the tissue using the soft
 > fiber-free tissue paper, apply 200-300 µL of Odyssey blocking
 > buffer without letting the tissue dry, and incubate for 30 minutes
 > at RT in the moist chamber. Wash with 1x TBS for 3 min.

5. **Multiplex Antibody Incubation:** Prepare the primary antibody
 > cocktail. Ensure fluorophores match the LMD7 excitation
 > wavelengths:

hamber. Wash with 1x TBS for 3 min.

5. **Multiplex Antibody Incubation:** Prepare the primary antibody
 > cocktail. Ensure fluorophores match the LMD7 excitation
 > wavelengths:

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 **Antibody_ID** **Channel** **Manufacturer** **Catalog** **Clone** **Temperature** **Box** **Slot** **Volume** **Animal** Dilution
 ----------------- ------------- ------------------ ------------- ----------- ----------------- --------- ---------- ------------ ------------ ----------
 Hoechst 405 +4°C 1 9 

 pRb_1_1 488 Abcam ab300671 EPR17512 -20°C 3 F1-F5 6 150

 Ki-67_6_1 488 CST 20701S D3B5 +4°C 3 A7 3 300

 PanCK_1_1 555 eBioscience 41-9003-82 AE1/AE3 +4°C 3 F2 1.125 800

 PAX8_10_1 647 Abcam ab215953 EPR18715 -20°C 5 K1-K3 9 100

 PH3_2_1 750 CST 43185S D2C8 +4°C 5 B8 3 300

 Odyssey buffer 868.875 
 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incubate overnight at 4C in the moist chamber.

# 5. Scanning

1. **Post-Stain Washing:** Wash the slides 3 times for 3 minutes each
 > in 1x TBS.

2. **Mounting for Imaging:** Dry the area surrounding the tissue
 > section using soft, fiber-free tissue paper. Add 80-100 µL of 70%

des 3 times for 3 minutes each
 > in 1x TBS.

2. **Mounting for Imaging:** Dry the area surrounding the tissue
 > section using soft, fiber-free tissue paper. Add 80-100 µL of 70%
 > glycerol in TBS per section and carefully apply a coverslip,
 > avoiding air bubbles.

3. **Fluorescence Scanning & Digital Imaging:** Perform fluorescence
 > scanning of the stained sections, ensuring fluorophores match the
 > LMD7 excitation wavelengths.

4. **Decoverslipping:** Gently remove the coverslip by submerging the
 > slide and decoverslip using 1x TBS.

# 6. Final Processing & LCM Collection

1. **Post-Stain Washing:** Wash the slides 3 x 3 minutes in 1x TBS.

2. **Transport & Drying:** To maintain tissue integrity, transport the
 > slides to the FIMM facility while they remain submerged in TBS.
 > Once on-site, gently air-dry the slides immediately prior to
 > loading them into the LMD7 system.

3. **Microdissection & Collection:** Microdissect target areas
 > comprising 8000 µm². Collect the dissected elements directly into
 > dry wells of 96-well plate.

**LCM Experimental Setup & Collection Conditions**

*Note on Plate Selection: While 4ti-0470 plates were used previously for
sample collection, 4ti-0960/C plates are being tested in this run based
on Otto's recommendation.*

**Slide Layout**

- **1x Empty PEN Slide (AR Control):** Used to control for membrane

collection, 4ti-0960/C plates are being tested in this run based
on Otto's recommendation.*

**Slide Layout**

- **1x Empty PEN Slide (AR Control):** Used to control for membrane
 > detachment during the antigen retrieval step.

- **1x Empty PEN Slide (Membrane Blank):** Used to generate blank
 > membrane samples via laser ablation.

- **2x PEN Slides with Tissue:** Used for the actual experimental
 > laser capture of the biological regions of interest.

**Collection Plates (4titude)**

To compare the performance of the two plate types and identify the
origin of polymer contamination, collections will be divided into the
following conditions:

**Membrane Controls (PEN-Only)**

- **Condition A: PEN-Only Plate (4ti-0750)**

 - **Contents:** Laser-ablated material from the empty PEN slide,
 > alongside designated empty wells.

 - **Purpose:** Serves as a baseline control for FIMM to identify
 > background signals specifically generated by the PEN polymer
 > within the standard plate.

- **Condition B: PEN-Only Plate (4ti-0960/C)**

 - **Contents:** Laser-ablated material from the empty PEN slide,
 > alongside designated empty wells.

 - **Purpose:** Serves as a baseline control for FIMM to identify
 > background signals specifically generated by the PEN polymer
 > within the newly recommended plate.

**Experimental Samples (PEN-LCM)**

- **Condition C: PEN-LCM Plate (4ti-0750)**

ackground signals specifically generated by the PEN polymer
 > within the newly recommended plate.

**Experimental Samples (PEN-LCM)**

- **Condition C: PEN-LCM Plate (4ti-0750)**

 - **Contents:** The actual laser-captured microdissected (LCMD)
 > tissue samples, alongside designated empty wells.

 - **Purpose:** Primary experimental collection using the standard
 > plate.

- **Condition D: PEN-LCM Plate (4ti-0960/C)**

 - **Contents:** The actual laser-captured microdissected (LCMD)
 > tissue samples, alongside designated empty wells.

 - **Purpose:** Primary experimental collection using the
 > recommended plate.

**Environmental Control**

- **Condition E: Empty Plate**

 - **Contents:** A completely empty 4titude plate that is sealed
 > immediately without any sample collection.

 - **Purpose:** Acts as a strict environmental and handling control
 > for FIMM to rule out ambient contaminants or background noise
 > originating from the plate materials or room environment.
